light_pressure_read.read: Light and pressure do not strongly push the outlook either way.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.

generated_text.quick_read: The Toccoa River is offering fair to good trout fishing conditions with recent stocking enhancing opportunities. Current river conditions show moderate flow and stable weather with light winds and partial skies.
generated_text.best_window: The best window is day 2 morning because stable light and pressure conditions combined with recent stocking create prime feeding conditions for trout in runs, seams, and structure.
generated_text.current_conditions: Flow is 239 cfs with a gage height of 1.24 ft. Water temperature and turbidity are unavailable.
generated_text.weather_setup: Skies are partly cloudy with about 29% average cover. Pressure is steady at 30.08 inHg with no strong trend. Winds are light at 3.4 mph from the west and there is a 63% chance of light rain totaling around 0.1 inches.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will favor comfortable feeding lanes near cover and structure, especially in runs and seams. Use a balanced nymph approach with clean drifts and smaller natural patterns. Include stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and Duracell nymphs to match recent stocking; avoid wading safety claims.
